key_points: Pastoralists are people who move with their herds (sheep, goats, cattle) to find fresh grazing grounds (pastures).
Movement is usually seasonal: In the Himalayas, nomads move to high mountains in summer and come down to low hills in winter.
Key Indian pastoralist groups include the Gujjar Bakarwals (J&K), Gaddi shepherds (Himachal Pradesh), and Raikas (Rajasthan).
In Africa, more than half the world's pastoral population lives, including groups like the Maasai, Somali, and Boran.
Colonial laws hurt pastoralists by turning grazing lands into cultivated farms to collect more land revenue.
The Forest Acts classified many grazing lands as 'Reserved,' meaning pastoralists could no longer enter them freely.
The Criminal Tribes Act (1871) classified many nomadic communities as 'criminals by nature' to monitor and control their movement.
A 'Grazing Tax' was introduced by the British, requiring pastoralists to pay for every animal they took to the fields.
key_terms: Bhabar: A dry forested area at the foothills of the Himalayas where nomads stay in winter.
Bugyal: Vast natural pastures in the high mountains (above 12,000 feet) used in summer.
Kharif: The autumn crop, usually harvested between September and October.
Rabi: The spring crop, usually harvested after March.
Transhumance: The practice of moving livestock from one grazing ground to another in a seasonal cycle.
important_groups: Gujjar Bakarwals: Move between Jammu (winter) and Kashmir (summer) with their goats and sheep.
Dhangars: An important pastoral community of Maharashtra who grow bajra and graze sheep.
Maasai: A large nomadic tribe in East Africa whose grazing lands were cut in half by international borders.
exam_tips: Remember the 'Seasonal Cycle': Move UP in summer, move DOWN in winter. This avoids extreme cold and finds fresh grass.
Understand why the British wanted pastoralists to settle: Nomads are hard to track, tax, and control compared to settled farmers.
Focus on the 'Waste Land Rules': The British saw grazing land as 'waste' because it didn't produce crops or tax money.
Identify the impact of the 'Criminal Tribes Act': It forced nomads to live in notified settlements and restricted their travel.
Note the 'Maasai' issue: In Africa, the best grazing lands were taken for 'Game Reserves' like Serengeti and Maasai Mara.
